feed.title: Kyhle Öhlinger
feed.subtitle: Hi all, My name is Kyhle Öhlinger and this blog post forms part of my personal blog. If you enjoy any of the posts, feel free to reach out and let me know :)

entry.title: High-Level Overview of my Current AI Toolset

link.title: High-Level Overview of my Current AI Toolset

entry.summary: I have been playing with artificial intelligence (AI) Chatbots on-and-off for the past 2 years which resulted in a few blog posts regarding my thoughts, usage, and completed CTFs. At the start of February, I set myself a goal to learn more about the hype around AI, Chatbots, Agents, etc. The first thing I learned about was how I can start creating chatbots effectively and from there I dove into...

entry.title: How to Build Your Own Chatbots with AI

link.title: How to Build Your Own Chatbots with AI

entry.summary: Chatbots have come a long way from early rule-based systems. Today’s generative AI and LLMs - like OpenAI’s GPT-4 - make it possible to build intelligent, responsive chatbots with minimal coding. This blog post was created to showcase the method that Jason Haddix has been implementing in his own personal methodology as described in the OWASP 2024 Keynote: Red, Blue, and Purple AI. If you haven...

entry.title: Querying Vertex AI Model Usage through GCP Observability Metrics

link.title: Querying Vertex AI Model Usage through GCP Observability Metrics

entry.summary: I have been playing with different GCP services recently, including GCP’s Vertex AI. During testing, I was trying to identify the usage of specific LLM model types within my environment through the GCP Logs Explorer which, unfortunately, did not return any results: GCP - Logs Explorer As per Google’s documentation, you need to enable data access logs to view the usage of any data read/write ...

entry.title: WithSecure AI Challenge - My LLM Doctor

link.title: WithSecure AI Challenge - My LLM Doctor

entry.summary: WithSecure recently released My LLM Doctor - a generative AI (GenAI) security challenge: WithSecure - MyLLMDoctor The purpose of this CTF is to experiment with multi-chain prompt injection which WithSecure recently released a blog post on. The promptingguide.ai defines multi-chain prompts as follows: To improve the reliability and performance of LLMs, one of the important prompt engineeri...

entry.title: From Curiosity to Creativity: My Thoughts on Prompt Engineering

link.title: From Curiosity to Creativity: My Thoughts on Prompt Engineering

entry.summary: Over the last few months, I have been attempting to learn more about Large Language Models (LLMs) and, as a result, I stumbled across prompt engineering and prompt engineering attacks. To improve my own knowledge of different LLM models and their susceptibility to attack, I have completed a few CTFs including ones from Wiz and WithSecure. If you haven’t seen them already, I have writeups for bo...
